recommendations.industry_observation: AngularJS reached End-of-Life on December 31, 2021. The industry has fully migrated to modern Angular (v2+) or React for enterprise applications.
recommendations.why_suggested: The architecture diagram on Page 12 and the lab instructions on Page 14 explicitly teach the outdated two-way data binding ($scope) specific to AngularJS 1.x.
recommendations.priority_rationale: High priority is based on a 95/100 score: critical lifecycle risk (EOL), frequent usage throughout the module, and inclusion in graded lab activities.
recommendations.suggested_faculty_action: Replace the AngularJS architecture lessons with modern React hooks or Angular 18 component-based architecture. Update all lab exercises to use modern frameworks.
specific_recommendations: Update the diagram on Page 12 to show unidirectional data flow (React) or modern Angular component trees.
Rewrite the 'Setting up the App' lab on Page 14 to use Vite instead of 'npm install angular@1.6'.

recommendations.industry_observation: Subversion (SVN) has been almost entirely replaced by Git in modern software development workflows.
recommendations.why_suggested: The text on Page 5 references using SVN for version control during team projects.
recommendations.priority_rationale: Medium priority (75/100): High lifecycle risk as it's obsolete in the industry, but lower frequency and not part of a specific graded lab.
recommendations.suggested_faculty_action: Replace references to SVN with Git and GitHub. Introduce basic Git commands (clone, commit, push, pull) instead.
specific_recommendations: Change the 'Version Control' section on Page 5 from SVN to Git.
Update the team project guidelines to require a shared GitHub or GitLab repository.

recommendations.industry_observation: While jQuery is still present in legacy systems, modern Vanilla JavaScript (ES6+) and component frameworks have eliminated the need for it in new projects.
recommendations.why_suggested: Page 38 briefly mentions using jQuery for DOM manipulation in a supplementary reading section.
recommendations.priority_rationale: Low priority (45/100): It is only mentioned once as supplementary reading and is not part of the core labs.
recommendations.suggested_faculty_action: Consider updating the supplementary reading to focus on Vanilla JS `document.querySelector`.
specific_recommendations: Replace the jQuery `$` syntax example on Page 38 with standard ES6 `document.querySelector`.
